page: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-64GB is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 300 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance characteristics. While single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the bottom 10%, the 32-core architecture delivers competitive multi-threaded performance. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU extended instructions, and PassMark CPU encryption tests all rank in the top 25%. Conversely, memory write bandwidth for large blocks is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, though read-only memory bandwidth remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of physical cores relative to its memory allocation, making it a viable option for parallelizable workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, cryptography tasks, and static web serving. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or memory-write-intensive databases due to its specific hardware and performance tradeoffs.
description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-64GB is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 32 vCPUs across 32 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 300 GB local SSD. While single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write bandwidth are relatively weak, the server excels in multi-threaded operations. It achieves strong benchmark results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, CPU extended instructions, and encryption tests. This hardware profile offers a high density of physical cores, making it a cost-effective choice for parallel processing tasks. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, cryptographic processing, and static web serving where high core counts can be fully leveraged without requiring excessive memory capacity.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-64GB is a compute-optimized KVM server featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 300 GB SSD storage, delivering strong multi-threaded performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-64GB is a compute-optimized server with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring thirty-two physical AMD cores and bundled SSD storage for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs
Includes 64.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 300 GB local SSD
Strong performance in multi-threaded decompression, encryption, and extended instructions
Lower single-core performance and large-block memory write bandwidth
Appropriate for parallel processing, software compilation, and static web serving
